label: Studio Soft
description: Light Next.js starter with a soft warm palette, generous whitespace, and quietly designed surfaces. Best when the prompt sounds creative or design-curious but has not committed to a real product, brand, or content shape yet.
signatureMotif: soft warm light surfaces, generous whitespace, and quiet designer-leaning baseline
promptHints: Treat the page as a calm light studio canvas — generous whitespace, gentle warmth, and one soft primary accent.
Stay extension-friendly: avoid committing to a strong product or brand voice unless the prompt clearly asks for it.
Prefer airy single-column rhythm and small descriptive cards over dense dashboards or marketing collages.

layouts: Use a centered single-column shell with a narrow max-width, slim transparent top bar, and tall vertical rhythm separating soft section blocks.
Build the hero as a gently centered or left-aligned headline stack with one calm primary action, one ghost link, and abundant breathing room around it.
Compose feature areas as a three-card row of soft pastel surfaces with rounded corners, short copy, and small icons sized as quiet markers rather than illustrations.
Lay out content or about pages as a single reading column with generous line-height, small caption-style metadata, and quiet horizontal rules between blocks.
Use forms and onboarding screens as a centered card with rounded corners, gentle shadow, and stacked fields with calm helper text underneath.
motifs: Pair off-white warm backgrounds with cream cards, 1px low-contrast borders, and a soft dusty primary accent that never shouts.
Keep typography quiet with Inter throughout, modest size jumps, and slightly heavier line-height so the page feels like a carefully spaced studio note.
Use sparse soft chrome: faint section dividers, restrained hover fills, gentle 1.1rem radii, and almost no decorative background treatment beyond a subtle gradient wash.
Let spacing and rhythm carry the design — predictable gutters, balanced negative space, and small rounded chips when categorisation is needed.
antiPatterns: Avoid bright SaaS gradients, oversized hero headlines, or marketing spectacle that turns this calm starter into a launch landing page.
Never lean on dark surfaces, neon accents, or high-contrast brutalist edges; the variant relies on warmth, softness, and quiet contrast.
Avoid dense dashboards, multi-column data grids, or product-feature stacks before the prompt clearly demands an actual product surface.
Do not pile glassmorphism, drop shadows, and gradients on the same surface; pick one quiet elevation cue so the studio softness stays believable.

themeTokens.bodyBackgroundImage: radial-gradient(circle at top left, color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-primary) 6%, transparent) 0%, transparent 38%), radial-gradient(circle at bottom right, color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-accent) 8%, transparent) 0%, transparent 42%)
